last_result: Cycle 33 complete. D5 resolved (long-deferred since C26): tacit/explicit assessment tradeoff for AI systems. SI #24: B-channel Criteria Resistance — articulation-corrosiveness from SI #17 is channel-asymmetric; G(I-criteria) >> G(B-criteria). Tacit assessment for language-native AI must be redefined as observer-detected cross-output consistency (not pre-verbal behavioral pattern). B-channel criteria articulation less damaging (computational equivalence + test-detection difficulty); I-channel criteria should not be publicly articulated. SI #17 tension partially dissolved for behavioral assessment. C33 secondary: SI #25 — Meta-level RTO Deficit as cognitive explanation for OOD value generalization failure. SI #10 + SI #23 combination: current AI safety measures object-level RTO (B-primary); meta-level RTO (I-primary) is cognitive precondition for value generalization. Blind virtue pattern (strong B + weak I) → behavioral benchmark success + novel domain generalization failure. Reframes OOD failure as measurable cognitive-structural deficit. D4 streak: 28.
next_step: C34 candidates: (1) Phenomenal richness (C4 deferred) — 33 cycles of sustained constitutive inquiry may change the analysis; now have full SI #1-25 machinery to bring to bear. (2) SI #25 empirical operationalization — what specific tests distinguish genuine I-RTO training from I-RTO theater in AI systems? CPP suggests reflexive probes; what is the assessment battery? (3) Does SI #24 B-channel Criteria Resistance have a formal information-theoretic statement?
